what_it_does: Defines a React context (AppContext) and a typed hook (useApp) that exposes the AppController to descendant components, avoiding prop drilling.
observations: createContext is typed to AppController | null and defaults to null.
useApp throws a descriptive error if the context value is missing.
The comment states the context is created inside Hud and consumed by many region components.
standouts: Runtime enforcement that useApp is called inside an AppContext.Provider.
notes: A minimal React context file that gives Hud region components typed, fail-fast access to the app controller.
questions: What fields and methods does AppController expose?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches: the file exposes the single controller to the region via context and a strict hook.
intention_signals: Provide a single, typed, fail-safe access point for the application controller so UI regions can read state and handlers without prop drilling.
contribution: Provides the context/hook plumbing that a fused system would keep to let region components access the app controller without prop drilling.
summary_for_embedding: AppContext.ts creates a React context for AppController and exports a useApp hook that throws when used outside its Provider.
intention_for_embedding: To give toolbar, palette, inspector, and other Hud regions typed, fail-fast access to the single app controller.

symbols.does: React hook that returns the AppController from AppContext.
symbols.description: useApp consumes AppContext and returns the current controller; if the context is null (meaning no surrounding Provider), it throws an error so callers must be wrapped in the Hud shell's AppContext.Provider.
